recurring_issues.pattern: Title case headings and/or formatting violations (brand rule: sentence case only)
recurring_issues.impact: Direct brand rule violation; title case headings in item 56 are explicitly cited; item 52 brand FAIL at 21/100 likely includes this issue; 5 articles have incomplete brand review and may carry the same violation

cluster_recommendations.issue: Cluster-wide citation vacuum — zero named sources across all six articles
cluster_recommendations.fix: For every article, replace all instances of 'research shows,' 'studies suggest,' and bare year references with fully attributed citations. Each article needs a minimum of 3 named sources with author, institution, and year. Recommended sources for this cluster: Tomlinson & Imbeau (2010) 'Leading and Managing a Differentiated Classroom'; CAST UDL Guidelines (2018); Hattie (2009) 'Visible Learning' (effect size data for differentiation); RAND Education and Labor (2021) 'Examining the Content and Quality of Next Generation Assessments'; NWEA 2023 MAP Growth norms. Add an inline 'Sources' or 'References' section at article foot.

cluster_recommendations.issue: No extractable direct answer block — all six articles fail QAPEDS Gate 2 and are invisible to AIO, ChatGPT, and Perplexity
cluster_recommendations.fix: Add a 1–3 sentence canonical answer (40–60 words) within the first 150 words of every article. The block must stand alone without surrounding context. For example, 'differentiation in education' should open with a sentence-case H2 ('What is differentiation in education?') followed immediately by a self-contained definition paragraph. For how-to articles (item 55), the answer block should name 3–5 strategies in the opener. This single change is the highest-ROI structural edit available to the cluster.

cluster_recommendations.issue: Statistical density critically low cluster-wide — no data points AI engines can extract as factual hooks
cluster_recommendations.fix: Add a minimum of 3 attributed statistics per article. Prioritize: effect sizes from Hattie's meta-analysis for differentiation (d = 0.58 for feedback; d = 0.43 for classroom management); NWEA reading-level spread data (average 5th-grade classroom spans 6+ grade levels); CAST UDL adoption figures. Place at least one stat in the first 200 words and one per major H2 section. Format as: 'According to [Author, Year], [stat].' Never use a percentage without a source.

cluster_recommendations.issue: Zero practitioner experience signals — no named teachers, no classroom scenarios, no first-party Wayground data
cluster_recommendations.fix: Add at least one named teacher anecdote or attributed classroom example per article. Format: 'Ms. Torres, a 4th-grade teacher in Austin, uses tiered exit tickets to...' Alternatively, integrate first-party Wayground platform data ('Based on [X] teachers using Wayground's differentiated activity feature...'). For articles that discuss strategies (items 52, 56, 57), include a brief 'How it looks in practice' callout per strategy section. This addresses both E-E-A-T Experience and AI Uniqueness Signal sub-scores simultaneously.

cluster_recommendations.issue: 5 of 6 articles have no completed brand review; 1 confirmed brand FAIL; title case headings confirmed in at least 2 articles
cluster_recommendations.fix: Prioritize brand review completion for items 53–57 before any article publishes. Immediate audit checklist for all six: (1) Convert all title case headings to sentence case. (2) Remove any em dashes from body copy — zero tolerance. (3) Verify no article opens with a question. (4) Confirm no 'quiz tool' or 'Quizizz' language. (5) Remove any editing artifacts (item 53 has a visible draft note in body copy — this must be deleted before publish). Item 52 requires full rewrite of brand-violating sections given its 21/100 brand voice score.

# Cluster Audit: Differentiated Learning / Core
**6 articles | Avg GEO Composite: 51.2/100 | Avg QAPEDS: 5.22/10**

---

## Cluster overview

This cluster is uniformly below publishable quality. The average GEO composite of 51.2 sits well below the 70+ threshold for competitive AI citation readiness, and not a single article across all six clears the AIO, ChatGPT, or Perplexity platform readiness gates. The cluster's defining characteristic is a near-total absence of attributed evidence — every article relies on unattributed "research shows" language, which is the single most damaging pattern for AI citability and E-E-A-T simultaneously. Five of six articles also have incomplete brand reviews, meaning the true brand compliance picture is worse than the data currently reflects.
